Life came full circle today! ✨

A few years ago while I was preparing for UPSC, I was also tutoring for pocket money. One random day in the middle of one such tutions, I received a call asking if I'd be interested in tutoring their daughter who'll be attending her boards coming year. 

As I asked how they got to know me, she mentioned one of my students studying in the same school as their daughter and a teacher who saw my student performing well so suggested my name to her. 

Then I asked her good name.. only to hear it's @kanakalasuma 🙂 Thought it was a prank.. 😂

then reality hit me. It was really her. She invited me over.. Manas (Sumakka's daughter) approved. 

Sumakka used to send her own vehicle to pick & drop me just so I don't face issues commuting back & forth to Himayatnagar & Gachibowli. 

It continued for a few months.. and my tenure there was over. On my last day back home, she was in a shoot... She made sure she called me immediately.. only to tell me "You're just like my own family. I know you live in a hostel. Anyday you feel low or you need home, come to mine. The doors are always open for you.. never forget I'm here" 

And today when I met her again.. I told her how grateful I am for her kindness and how that pushed me to do better in my life. She asked what I'm doing these days..I showed her Dee_scribble. I could see she was happy.

While I was referring to my student that she grew up so much, Sumakka corrected me saying I was a kid myself.. She showed how kind someone could be and it's impact.. and through Deescribble I'm carrying it forward 🩷

Life is made of many stories.. and this one's close to my heart!!!

ITINERARY!!!!! I follow: 

I’ve reached Banaras by flight, train, and bus.
Honestly? Buses feel better. Flight is comfortable and easier. 

I usually stay in hostels near Chowki Ghat (within 300 m). Location matters more than luxury here.

How I do the temples:
Start with Kalbhairav Temple in the evening.
Aarti around 6:30 PM, don’t rush it.

Kashi Vishwanath early morning, around 4 AM.
Shayan Aarti at 10:30 PM  Everyone should experience this once.

Vishalakshi Shaktipeeth is right outside Vishwanath Temple 

Sakshi Ganpati, Dundi Ganpati, Chintamani Ganapati — I take darshan without fail.

Food (learn from my mistakes): Enjoy street food, but don’t overdo it.
Been there. Food poisoning happened. Moderation is devotion too. But Badam milk at night? Mandatory.
You’ll find it everywhere.

Shopping:
Best Banarasi sarees (top quality): Absolute fashion (yes, pricey). Contact no: 9506057066, 7860716785

For business / sourcing: Sanatana Weaves. Contact no.: +91 93916 96044

Go around the street shops. They have best sarees within 700-1500 range. They're beautiful too!

Walk it out: Walk from Harishchandra Ghat to Manikarnika Ghat.
All ghats are connected, but this stretch feels safer and deeply grounding.

Hydrate. Hydrate. Hydrate.
Cannot stress this enough.

Ganga Aarti tip:
 Aarti happens on almost every ghat between 6:30–7 PM.
Don’t overcrowd Dashashwamedh Ghat.
Choose a quieter ghat. Let it be peaceful, not performative.

Boat rides:
Calming. Meditative. Bargain. Always.
Most important: Don’t be a tourist. Be an explorer.
Walk more. Talk to strangers. Stay alert, not comfortable

And please !!!!!!
Don’t take cameras into temples.
They’re not your studios, for god’s sake.

Be respectful.
That place deserves it.
Come back happy, humbled, and safe. 🌊🕉️

#deecodingvaranasi #banaras #kashi

But being an explorer should always make you be a part of the place. That's what the story tells me. In the last two years, Kashi was in the boat with photos and Ganga Harthi videos. The attempt to steal that kiss was a decoding waranasi. That means the difference between a wonderlaki waranasi and a tourist was so big. The place was a place where you could travel and enjoy the place. We all wish to be able to enjoy it. My dear teacher, Tathagari Samanu's Chaganti Koteswarav, has given me the opportunity to share stories with my parents. I am very proud of them. I am very proud of the stories they tell me. I am proud of the stories they tell me. That's the story. May we all be responsible tourists and have the best time of our life.

Maybe this is your perspective,let me share mine, as a mother living it every day.

Motherhood, for me, has come with very real, quiet sacrifices like sleepless nights, mental load, and putting parts of myself on pause in ways I never imagined.
Not because I see it as a pedestal, but because love sometimes asks for it in ways you only truly understand when you’re in it, day after day.

Even with a nanny, I still don’t really have time for myself and that’s a choice I’ve made, because I want to be present for my son.

Sometimes it also means choosing to stay back because your child isn’t comfortable in new spaces, or because their routine, their feeding, their sense of security comes first… and I’m one of those moms.

At the same time, I feel we need to be a little careful about what we glorify or call a “setting a standard.”
Motherhood is already a journey where we’re constantly judged, told what’s right and what’s not, and made to feel like we’re falling short of some ideal.

For many of us, it doesn’t fit into a ‘no sacrifice’ narrative it’s messy, demanding, and full of quiet trade offs that aren’t always visible. Not idealised, just real.

I don’t think motherhood can be defined so easily without living it fully.
And I don’t think one ‘standard’ or one ‘generation’ of mothers is better than another we’re all just doing our best in very different realities.

At the end of the day, our motherhood isn’t measured by how it looks from the outside, but by the bond we build with our child the safety, the love, the connection only we understand.

And that version of motherhood deserves just as much respect and space 🤍

transcription: Kashikis are very smart. If someone likes someone very much, then they are the ones who have lost their loved ones. I found somebody in between that boy who used to always sit silently next to a temple. One day I spoke to him. Who are you? Who are you? Who are you? I didn't speak anything. After 1520 minutes of silence, he told me, I am a boy raised by a single mother. I lost my father when I was a child. My mother said that she had suffered a lot. I had studied hard to give a good life to my mother. And last year, she left me. I studied hard here. I didn't know what to do with this ambition from my father. I came here to tell him that I am close to my mother. Now I didn't know what to talk. Then I told him, I have suffered a lot. Why should I suffer? Definitely, you should do something good. You should make up. Proud. She is definitely looking over you. There was a moment of silence. And the boy started crying. And then I cried. He said, My mother can send you back. Or you can be my mother. I will definitely do something good. That is when I understood. God is somewhere. He is in our heart. If we see God in different people, some people see God in our heart. But all that we have to do is to be kind. I understood how powerful that is. And this moment is very close to my heart. We don't know what pain anyone is suffering. So let's be kind.

transcription: In Kashi, every man has a temple. I thought it was a joke. But no, it's so damn true. If you don't find four temples in a street, you just don't explore properly. Every man has a temple. We expect attention from that temple. But every time we see a temple, we slow down a little bit. We try not to have bad thoughts in our head. Even when we go to the temple, we try to behave normally. Why? Because you may be a little bit of fear God might be watching. If God is the same person, we don't have to act like a man. This place is a reminder that a person has to behave. But it's a reminder to me that I should be a better person even when nobody is watching. We can say that we are not a bad person. But we know our witness if we are doing right or wrong. We should not do anything wrong. But before we do anything, we should think and stop doing it. That is what this place stands for.

transcription: When I first came to Kashi, I only had 2 days to explore this place. So I asked locals how long it would take to shoot this place. They told me 7 days. Now I don't have much time. So I took a list from them and I started running. I covered every single day in 2 days. And I was super exhausted by the end of it. I was sitting in the Ganges in the last day. It was around level 1130 in the night of my second day. So there was a big crowd sitting next to me. I was talking to him. So he asked me, How many days did you come to this place? I told him I came for 2 days. He said it would take at least 7 days if I asked locals. But I covered this place in 2 days. And he told me, This means you were running throughout the time. You didn't actually understand the place. You just explored it. I really don't understand this place if I think about it. And he told me, If I do every single thing in my life, I would miss the meaning many times. Decisions, experiences, and that moved something inside me. So I decided to stay back for a few more days. On the other day, I understood what this person exactly told. I remember the moment I explored the place. And this just reminds me how important it is to slow down sometimes in your life.

transcription: For those who want to do better communication skills, I will tell you a technique that I have used. That is called Jam. Just a minute. Let me tell you how this works. Prioritize a topic, analyze it mentally, and talk about it in one minute. You can use a stopwatch to calculate that one minute. It can be done in one day or the first time. But if you practice it regularly, your brain will get used to prioritizing very important stuff first and neglect it later. You can talk about very important things first, and that too effortlessly in a very less amount of time. You can't talk about it too fast. All of this is for you to practice to talk meaningfully in a very less time. If you have any doubts about this, comment below. Bye!

transcription: If recruiters approach you in LinkedIn, there might be a big visibility issue. So let's talk about how to fix that. You will get the wrong idea. Recruiters will message you. If you don't send a connection request to the recruiter, make sure you draft a personalized message. How do you do that? Open their profile. See their achievements. See their new certifications. Hey, congratulations on this. Great work. Great certification. Would be happy to connect with you. This might work out a lot of times. Second thing. Occasionally, open their profile. Start leaving some meaningful comments. I just want to post a certification. Just saying, hey, congratulations on this. Minimum to Arson achievement. Hey, great. Something nice, something kind and something professional. Third thing. If I don't want a job, I won't be able to send messages like that. My profile matches your opening. Let me know how to proceed with this. Drive a professional message like that and send it. Number four. Whether you like it or not, this is what the profile picture looks like. Open to work. transcription: If you are not a user of LinkedIn profile, then it is not wrong to set up LinkedIn. So let's discuss how to set up your LinkedIn account. Let's start with basics. First thing, your profile picture. That is your first impression. That is why you should consider it to be very professional. And that is why it is called a professional headshot with a little bit of smile. So that you seem approachable. Second thing, your banner which is really very important. Many people fill it with quotes or sceneries they like. But instead what you can do is write a line about your expert skill. Like I am a content writer. I can write something like building hero content. Third one, the about section. Without any paragraphs, make it into three lines. What are you excited about? Next, your skill and then end it with what kind of opportunities you are open for. The fourth one, you don't even know that there are many people. Feature section. In that, you can write a post that you are very thoughtful. Or you can pin a skill. So when people open your LinkedIn profile, they scroll through and the first thing they see is this. And without looking at the other 20 posts, they will be able to figure out what kind of a person you are with your skill. Fifth one, most important. Keywords, recruiters, tech savvy, passionate designer, and a searcher. They search keywords. UX designer, copywriter, marketing strategist. So if you fill these keywords in your profile, when they search them, you will see the profile. Otherwise, your profile will go missing into the ocean of other lakhs of LinkedIn profiles. And finally, even if you set it up so well, it is very important to post regularly. And let's discuss how to do that in the next episode. Bye.

transcription: In my early corporate days, every new role and task introduced me to a concept. The fear of unknown. Colleagues say, don't mess up this task, it's too tough, people say it's not going to work. Some people say seniors are very strict and very careful. In such times, my mind goes directly to survival mode. So, what I thought at this time is two things. One, maybe this task is out of my league. Second thing, don't make mistakes. Because mistakes make you lose your job. This is the perception usually people kind of create or try to create. Or maybe all of this might be me over thinking. But when I think about it, every time I start with my research and start doing the work. It's not that hard to do the work. And I had a lot of fun working on things like that. I understood it immediately. My problem is not work, but to start the work. Every time you start a new role, new task, new project. Take a chill pill, sip your coffee and start that damn work. Because many times new experiences are not to be missed. They are to be learned in new things.

transcription: Do you know what is the best decision I have taken in my life? After my work trip, I went to Kashi without coming home. And that changed my life. Traveling doesn't change your life. But you can take the decision to travel. You can start your journey by packing your bag and packing your things. Your life will have a real turning point. All the fears of, Ugh! My office, My interpermission, Or something like that. All these things are at least once in your life. Try traveling solo. After that, Surely we are shifting your perspective about life. I will make a new experience for you. My first solo trip gave me the life I have today. Not about money. Something about that. It's about the clarity I have on myself. And how I am worth all the risk I take in my career. And the respect I demand on a table. Who so ever might be sitting on the other side of it. This is exactly why a lot of people talk about their first solo trip. A lot more than 100 more trips they have been in their entire life. Maybe it all starts from choosing yourself. Happy 100k guys!

transcription: This video is for those who want to achieve something in life. I told you in the house when I was a kid, I was wondering why you are living with them, why you are becoming like them. I started realizing that it's so damn true. The first thing is, if we start to hang out with people who are like us in our field, or people who know us more, we start understanding how the market is, so we can create an opportunity out of it. Second thing, content, editing, if you can't discuss the money with the people who are already doing that, you will get a lot more information, and that information will be practical to apply directly in life. So you are learning something better than textbooks and courses. And, not only professional skills, but also the set of challenges they faced, you will get an opportunity to know all of them. Because of that, when you face a similar situation, you can easily dodge it. You are going to save a lot of energy. The fourth major thing is, when you keep discussing these things with people who are already in your profession, this kind of becomes a conversation. You will be able to understand it like work. Because of that, you will build a habit of actually meeting more of such people, so you are learning even more than what you think. Even if I have my set of friends, I also make sure that professionally, I have those kind of friends who are from the similar kind of work, so that I am not making my professional life boring, and also growing at the same time. Because growth should be boring at the same time. Bye!

transcription: If I had given the opportunity to change my life by mistake, I would make sure that she would continue her education, do a job and prioritize herself in a very beautiful way. There are reasons for me to say this. Many times, we say that we glorify the suffering and sacrifice of our mothers. It only damages her and sets a standard for the next generations. That, you know, they sacrifice their mothers, they make them do the same to their children. Did they raise their parents? They are the mothers who are living in the world. I feel that their life is still very long. One day, before I went to the hostel, my mother always said that she would take a promise from my hand. That I would never see a day in my life where I fetch my hand before someone for money. Here, the topic is not about money, it's about dignity and selfrespect. If she had not had any other choice in her life, she wanted that for me. And she has supported me throughout the time. Sometimes, I know that she would help me if I knew that she would help me. You know, the guilt they carry is the one that they remove from our lives. So, the two things we have to do now is, First thing, we should take care of our mothers very carefully. That is, not only to take them to the shops, but also to make sure that they are not against the dignity and selfrespect. You have to make sure that you are going against the entire world. Second thing, tomorrow even we are going to get married. We might also have kids. Tomorrow, our children will be like this. The next generation has to witness strong, healthy, happy moms. Because why not? Standards shift Avali. It's high time.

transcription: 2 days ago I've had the worst anxiety attack. That day I felt that we should take mental health as granted. For some years I was diagnosed with clinical depression and anxiety. Every little thing was very painful. I understood the issue clearly in that journey. There is no cure for it. We just live with it. We live in a very strong way. Or else we feel like we are cured. But sometimes we are tested by time. We feel that everything is fine. And we feel that we are in extreme negative emotions. Let's start the story again. We are not in our situation. We tend to get overprotective about ourselves. And we are always attentive to double check if everything is going right. But we should remember one thing in times like this. This is not the same life. In this life we also laughed, smiled, happy, ate, liked, and knew with friends. We saw successful moments. We saw so many things. We feel like we are in a moment of happiness. This is not how my life is going to end. In the country there is always a tomorrow and it's going to be beautiful.

#deescribble #deetales #mostlydeecoded
transcription: This video is for those who feel uncomfortable to say a single word. When our time is good, everyone is fine with us. So, I used to have a lot of friends. Even if there were so many friends, a lot of times I used to feel lonely and left out. And, our friends are always more important to us in our bad times. In one of those bad times, I was left all alone. I never came to talk to anyone. Even when I felt very sad at first, later, slowly I realized that it wasn't their fault. It was my fault. In the other times, I didn't say no to the things I'm not comfortable with. Because, I never said no to anything other than the things I adjusted to them. Even to the behavior that I didn't like. And, after this, I started saying no to a lot of things. I didn't have any interest in conversations. I didn't have any interest in any of the things I was interested in. I didn't have any interest in any of the places I went to. This saved a lot of energy and this is one reason why I didn't even have the clarity to go beyond myself. Because, the boundaries are clearly set. Even after all this, I was alone for a lot of time. But, I never felt lonely.

But that job introduced me to a word. Toxic work culture. If you finish your 9 hours job in 9 hours, then there is no value for ideas, no respect for work, no over time drama. So in that month, I decided I should leave the job. So all the people I know are too early, you shouldn't leave the job, you should be adjusted, there are only corporates like that. But I chose myself. That decision felt like I was coming back home. I started sleeping better, my life started getting better, I started living normally. So in these times, it's not about me leaving a job, it's more about me saving myself from that kind of harm. Adi, you can leave it. Today I am working at a place where my current manager emphasizes more on personal wellbeing. When you say, a job is too much of an interweaving for your life, and you are not able to live a personal life, you should take that as a sign. Because a job is just a job, not your entire life.

transcription: Seminar starts but you get free. You don't talk in the middle of the interview. All of this is not bad luck. This is bad communication skills. I will share 4 workouts with you. 1. If you are confident in body language, then you are saved. 3. Imitate. 2. Listen. Observe. You should start using your daily routine. You will get more practice. 4. Own it. If you practice consistently, you will get very comfortable. And always remember, comfort in a skill equals confidence. That's how you gain confidence and that is very important for communication skills. First, everyone will do pentagy. I did it too. If you are doing practice, you will surely get better at it. Good luck.

transcription: I thought the relationships were over but I got a new story. My dad and I planned my life route map beautifully. But we didn't know life had something else planned for us. UPSC. My dad's dream, my responsibility, vision boards, 5A alarms, mock tests, I could do everything. Just... ...understand that. In such a time, I thought my dad was honest and I confronted him. Dad, I can't do this. And yes, he was hurt, he was confused. But he had a sense of what I wanted to explain. But this time, not with my words, but with my actions. Slowly he saw it, the progress I was making. And most importantly, he saw how happy I was doing what I liked. That's when the story changed. If I cut it, today, he's my biggest cheerleader. And again, we're walking on the same path together. Because a no didn't break our bond.

transcription: Do you know what is the worst enemy of our ego? It's rejection. I personally take rejections as my own. He was told on his face that you don't have creative imagination. Second one, the world's greatest host, Oprah. She was told she was unfit to be on TV. And Michael Jordan, who plays the role of basketball, was not selected into his school team. Just imagine. The school people told him, you are not good enough to Michael Jordan. But they didn't have the right to do it. They had to help people to get better positions. The rejections and the judgments they had made, most of the times they are feedbacks to work on yourself. Sometimes from them and sometimes from life. So if you face any rejection, just remember that we are a work in progress to become the best version of ourselves.

caption:
You won’t understand it now… but one day, it’ll all make sense. Even the mess. Even the delays!!! - SteveJobs about connecting the dots 🙌🔥
 
#deetales #deescribble
transcription: Do you ever feel stuck in your life? Because I feel that sometimes. Then, Steve Jobs said a powerful statement to us. That you cannot connect the dots looking forward. You can only connect them looking backwards. So, let me explain this to you. Steve Jobs was a college dropout. He has no plan, no security. The only thing that was driving him was his curiosity. He started to learn the calligraphy of his interest randomly. A few years later, that was a random class. We are now at the foundation of the Mac. He didn't even know that he was building something incredible for the history. You also feel that your journey is missing sometimes, right? But I don't think that is something that you have to worry about. Until you are putting in consistent work into your dreams and goals. Because one day it will all connect back and it will all start making sense. You will realize that every delay, every time struggle, any waste of a little one. So, don't wait for the time to be perfect. Start missing, start scared, but just start.
